seo.pageTitle: Tour Seoraksan y Sokcho desde Seúl: Parque Nacional y Playa | AtoC
seo.metaDescription: Tour de un día desde Seúl al Parque Nacional Seoraksan (UNESCO) y Sokcho: cablecar + senderismo → Mercado Pesquero Sokcho (marisco fresco) → Playa de Sokcho. 14-16 horas. Grupo pequeño.
seo.title: Tour Seoraksan y Sokcho desde Seúl: Parque y Playa | AtoC
seo.description: Seoul small-group day trip to Seoraksan: Seoraksan National Park trails → Sokcho Tourist Fishery Market → Sokcho Beach. 12 hours, certified guide, small group. Autumn foliage peak October.

catalog_card.title: Tour Seúl→Seoraksan: Parque Nacional UNESCO, Mercado de Sokcho y Playa
catalog_card.shortCardDescription: Excursión intensa de un día desde Seúl al Parque Nacional Seoraksan (Reserva de la Biosfera UNESCO, las montañas de granito más espectaculares de Corea) y la ciudad costera de Sokcho: cablecar hasta los 800 m + Templo Sinheungsa y Buda de Bronce → Mercado Pesquero de Sokcho (marisco de primera mano) → Playa de Sokcho (Mar del Este, arena blanca). 14-16 horas de ida y vuelta. Solo para quien quiera una jornada intensa.
catalog_card.subtitle: Las montañas de granito más espectaculares de Corea y el marisco más fresco del Mar del Este — en un día intenso desde Seúl.

headlineLine1: Montaña de granito por la mañana, Mar del Este al mediodía —
headlineLine2: Seoraksan y Sokcho en un día intenso desde Seúl

price.priceNote: Price not yet confirmed from GetYourGuide listing snippet. Once original price is set, AtoC Korea sale price applies a flat 10% discount.

hero.tagline: El parque nacional más espectacular de Corea y el marisco más fresco del Mar del Este — todo en un día desde Seúl

itineraryStops.name: Salida desde Seúl
itineraryStops.description: Salida muy temprana desde Seúl (05:30-06:00) para llegar a Seoraksan antes del mediodía: **Salida 8 de Hongik University (Hongdae)** o **Salida 4 de Myeongdong**. El autocar toma la **Autopista Seoul-Yangyang** y el **Túnel Inje-Yangyang (10,96 km, uno de los más largos de Asia)** para llegar a Seoraksan en ~2,5 horas. ⚠️ **Este tour implica una jornada de 14-16 horas** — no apto para quienes prefieren días relajados.
highlights: **14-16 horas de jornada total** — solo para los más activos
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Hotel-area pickup keeps the day's logistics simple. Departing by 07:30 ensures you arrive at Seoraksan before the 10:30 cable car peak rush — saving 1-2 hours of queueing in the autumn high season.

smartNotes.tip: Bring a light layer — coach AC can run cool. Restroom break ~halfway at a service area en route. Window seat on left side gives the best Taebaek mountain views before the tunnel.

_poi_meta.note: Operational pickup stop — Seoul departure (variable hotel/station pickup); not a sightseeing POI

itineraryStops.name: Parque Nacional Seoraksan
itineraryStops.category: Reserva de la Biosfera UNESCO (1982) — Las montañas de granito más espectaculares de Corea
itineraryStops.description: **Parque Nacional Seoraksan (설악산국립공원)** — designado **Parque Nacional n.° 5** en 1970 y **Reserva de la Biosfera UNESCO en 1982** — es considerado el parque más espectacular de Corea por sus masas de granito (el pico Daecheongbong a **1.708 m**), cascadas y bosques de abeto. El tour visita el **sector de Outer Seorak (외설악)**: **(1) Templo Sinheungsa (신흥사, fundado 652 CE)** — uno de los templos más antiguos de Corea, rodeado de arces y abetos con el **Gran Buda de Bronce Tongil de 14 m** (inaugurado 1997, dedicado a la reunificación) al fondo; **(2) Cablecar de Seoraksan** (~5 min hasta los **800 m de altitud**, estación superior **Gwongeumseong**, fortaleza de piedra del siglo IX con vistas panorámicas a los picos de granito y el Mar del Este — en días despejados). **Entrada al parque ₩5.000 + cablecar opcional ~₩18.000-20.000** (entrada incluida; cablecar a cargo del visitante o incluido según la reserva — confirmar al reservar).
highlights: **Reserva de la Biosfera UNESCO (1982)** — el único parque nacional UNESCO de Corea
**Cablecar hasta 800 m** → Gwongeumseong (fortaleza siglo IX) con vistas al Mar del Este
**Templo Sinheungsa** (652 CE) + **Buda de Bronce de 14 m** (1997)
Pico Daecheongbong (**1.708 m**) visible desde el cablecar en días despejados
timeUsed: Cablecar subida (5 min) + Gwongeumseong + bajada — ~60-75 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The 2.5-hour drive each way is justified only by the centerpiece: Korea's 5th national park (1970) and the country's only UNESCO Biosphere Reserve (1982) — combined with the world's largest seated bronze Buddha and the world's oldest Zen Buddhist temple, all within a 15-minute walk of the Sogongwon entrance. Picked over Naksansa-temple alternatives because Seorakdong Entrance puts paved Sinheungsa + cable-car-ridge access + Biseondae stream trail within a single 3-hour window — no other Seoraksan stop combines all three with this short walking budget. Seoraksan's combined IUCN Green List + UNESCO Biosphere Reserve + tentative World Heritage status makes it **Korea's most-recognized natural-heritage park** — a credential that helps the day-trip earn its 5-hour round-trip drive cost in a way that pure-scenery alternatives (Odaesan, Jirisan) cannot match for first-time-Korea travelers seeking a single mountain anchor.

visitBasics.hours: Park always open; Cable car typically 08:00-17:30 (weather-dependent); Sinheungsa Temple 06:00-sunset
visitBasics.closed: Open year-round; some trails may close periodically for restoration
visitBasics.admission: Park entrance FREE (was 3,500 KRW until recent abolition); Cable car RT Adult 16,000 / Child 12,000 KRW (on-site only, no advance booking)
visitBasics.walking: Easy paths at base (Sinheungsa, cable-car base); moderate to challenging hikes (Biseondae 3 km, Ulsanbawi steep stairs, Heundeulbawi 1.5 hr each way)

convenience.restroom: At main entrance area, cable car station, Sinheungsa Temple grounds, along major trails

smartNotes.photo: Tongildaebul (14.6 m bronze Buddha) is the iconic single shot. Cable car ride frames Sinheungsa + Ulsanbawi Rock + Sokcho City + East Sea. October foliage against granite peaks is world-famous.
smartNotes.facilities: Visitor center, cafes (Dahyang Cafe), restaurants, gift shops at Seorakdong Entrance. Free hiking equipment rental 9:00-14:00 (return by 16:00). Asiatic Black Bear Statue mascot near entrance.
smartNotes.tip: Cable car tickets ARE NOT bookable in advance — queues can exceed 1-2 hours in October. If skies are foggy, skip the cable car entirely (no view). Best 3-hour budget: Tongildaebul + Sinheungsa + Biseondae streamside walk. Wear sturdy closed-toe shoes — paths are rocky and damp in places.

sources: Korea Tourism Organization (VisitKorea — Seoraksan National Park (Outer Seorak) listing + IUCN Green List confirmation)
IUCN Green List of Protected and Conserved Areas (iucngreenlist.org — Seoraksan official entry + 2014 designation + 398.24 km² area)
Korea National Park Service (KNPS — Seoraksan National Park Office official site + endangered species management plan)
UNESCO World Heritage Centre (Mt. Sorak Biosphere Reserve 1982 designation + tentative World Heritage listing)
Wikipedia (Seoraksan National Park — corpus on 30 peaks, 2,000+ animal species, 1,400+ plant species)

itineraryStops.name: Mercado Turístico Pesquero de Sokcho
itineraryStops.category: El mercado de marisco más fresco del Mar del Este — almuerzo en el puerto de Sokcho
itineraryStops.description: **Mercado Turístico Pesquero de Sokcho (속초관광수산시장)** — mercado de marisco y productos frescos en el corazón de Sokcho. La ciudad de Sokcho es uno de los principales puertos pesqueros del Mar del Este, con capturas diarias de **gamba del norte (양미리), cangrejo de aguas profundas, calamar y erizo de mar**. En el mercado se puede elegir el marisco en la planta baja y pedirlo preparado en el piso superior (~₩20.000-40.000/persona). También famoso por el **Ojingeo-sun (오징어순대, calamar relleno de carne)** y el **Dakgangjeong (닭강정, pollo crujiente dulce-picante)**, la especialidad callejera local.
highlights: **Gamba del norte, cangrejo, calamar y erizo** — los más frescos del Mar del Este
Marisco elegido en planta baja, preparado al momento en planta 1
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The Sokcho market is the catalog's strongest mountain-and-ocean food pivot — within 10 minutes of leaving Seoraksan's mountain temple atmosphere, travelers eat the East Sea coast's most distinctive working-port lunch. Picked over Daepohang Port Raw Fish Street alternatives because the market's all-weather indoor structure plus the takeaway-friendly dakgangjeong format absorbs typical small-group group dynamics (mixed seafood preferences) without forcing a sit-down raw-fish commitment. Beyond mere dining, the market is the entry point to **Korea's only remaining collective settlement of displaced Korean War people** — Abai Village, accessible via the 200-KRW gaetbae hand-pulled ferry across the 92m Cheongho Lake channel — where Hamheung-style cold noodles, abai sundae, ojingeo sundae, and gajami sikhae form a North-Korean-diaspora food map preserved nowhere else in South Korea.

visitBasics.hours: Most stalls 09:00-21:00 daily; individual stall hours vary
visitBasics.closed: Open year-round; individual stalls may close on Sundays or rotating off-days
visitBasics.walking: Easy — flat downtown market; comfortable shoes for ~30-90 min wandering

convenience.parking: Underground parking 600 KRW first 30 min, 400 KRW per 10 min thereafter, max 12,000 KRW/day

smartNotes.photo: Live-fish tanks at the Raw Fish Center; squid sundae frying on the pan; the multi-colored 'SOKCHO' staircase at the central entrance; the Hyuntaenae stall #12 yellow signage; rainbow-colored rice cakes and gimbap.
smartNotes.facilities: Sit-down restaurants behind many street-food stalls. Hanaro Mart for take-home gifts (dried fish, dried mackerel, dried pollack). Multilingual signs.
smartNotes.tip: Bring CASH 10,000-20,000 KRW per person — many vendors don't accept foreign cards. Try the dakgangjeong + squid sundae pair as the must-eat combo. Avoid eating free samples without intent to purchase (Korean market etiquette). Visit before 12:30 to skip the lunchtime peak.

sources: Korea Tourism Organization (VisitKorea — Sokcho Tourist & Fishery Market listing + Top 3 Eateries Dakgangjeong/Raw Fish/Abai Village)
Sokcho City Tourism Office (sokcho.go.kr — official market re-naming history Jungang → Tourist Fishery)
AJU Press / Korea Times (January 22, 2026 — Abai Village displaced-people heritage feature article)
Visit Gangwon (gangwon.to — official provincial tourism portal on Sokcho market specialties)
Wikipedia (Sokcho — corpus on Korean War Hamgyeong-province refugee settlement history + Abai Village formation)

itineraryStops.name: Playa de Sokcho
itineraryStops.category: Mar del Este — arena blanca con Seoraksan al fondo
itineraryStops.description: **Playa de Sokcho (속초해수욕장)** — playa del **Mar del Este** abierta desde **1976**, a 5 min del Mercado Pesquero. La franja de arena de **1,7 km** con el perfil de los picos de granito de Seoraksan al fondo es el escenario fotográfico que combina montaña y océano en un solo encuadre. El **Mar del Este** (동해) es significativamente más frío que el Mar Amarillo (Jeju/oeste) pero más limpio y azul. En verano, el área de baño está habilitada. El paseo por la orilla con Seoraksan al fondo es el momento más memorable del tour.
highlights: Playa de **1,7 km** de arena blanca con **Seoraksan al fondo** — montaña + océano en un encuadre
**Mar del Este** — más frío y azul que el Mar Amarillo
Zona de baño habilitada en verano (julio-agosto)
El encuadre montaña-océano más único de Corea
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Sokcho Beach is the day's third pillar — completing the Seoraksan mountain → Sokcho fishery → East Sea coast triad that only Korea's east coast offers in a single day-trip. Picked over Naksan Beach to the south because Sokcho Beach is walkable from the Tourist Fishery Market and the Express Bus Terminal, eliminating an extra 15-min drive. The **Sokcho Eye Ferris Wheel** elevates Sokcho Beach beyond a generic East-Sea shore — its 65m height + transparent acrylic walls + 16-minute ride deliver a panoramic that captures Seolak Bridge / Geumgang Bridge / Abai Village / Sokcho Lighthouse Observatory / Yeonggeumjeong Pavilion / Dongmyeong Port / Jodo Island / Seoraksan in a single circular sequence, completing the day's mountain-and-ocean narrative with one final aerial-perspective photograph that no other East Coast beach in Korea can match.

visitBasics.hours: Beach open 24 hours; swimming permitted only during officially-open season (July 4 - early-mid August 2025)
visitBasics.closed: Open year-round for walking; swimming closed September-June
visitBasics.admission: Free (parasol/lounger rentals 20,000-30,000 KRW during swim season)
visitBasics.walking: Easy — flat beach; pine forest park trail behind

smartNotes.photo: Sokcho Eye Ferris wheel from the beach is the iconic shot. Multi-colored 'SOKCHO' staircase at central entrance. T-shaped pier with metal Tree of Hearts. Sunrise over East Sea (especially New Year).
smartNotes.facilities: Restaurants and convenience shops along Haeoreum Road. Air/water pumps to clean shoes after sand. Photo zones with art installations.
smartNotes.tip: Best for swim experience: July 4 - early August (also offers night swim 19:00-21:00). Best for photo + walking: spring/autumn (mild weather, fewer crowds). The northern part (around Sokcho Eye) is busier than the southern part. Strong wind even in summer — bring a windbreaker. Pair with the New Year sunrise visit if traveling in late December / early January.

sources: Korea Tourism Organization (VisitKorea — Sokcho Beach official listing + Sokcho Eye dedicated listing with 65 m specs)
Sokcho City Tourism Office (sokcho.go.kr — official beach + Sokcho Eye operating-hours page)
Visit Gangwon (gangwon.to — provincial tourism portal on Sokcho coastal attractions)
Sokcho Eye Operator Site (Angelus Wish Theme Park — combo-ticket package + reservation system)
Korea Times / Yonhap News (March 2022 Sokcho Eye opening coverage + 'Korea's first beach Ferris wheel' designation)

itineraryStops.name: Regreso a Seúl
itineraryStops.description: Regreso a Seúl por la Autopista Seoul-Yangyang (~2,5 h con parada en área de descanso). Llegada a **Hongdae o Myeongdong ~22:00-22:30 h** según el tráfico. ⚠️ Este tour puede terminar pasadas las 22:00.
highlights: Parada en área de descanso de Autopista
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Return logistics — drop-off chosen for guest convenience and timing certainty; expressway route minimizes exposure to local traffic and ensures predictable arrival window.

smartNotes.tip: Autumn-foliage Sunday returns can run 30-60 min late — build buffer into evening dinner reservations. Keep snacks / water for the return drive.

_poi_meta.note: Operational stop (return); identified by name pattern. visitBasics/convenience were not factsheet-derived.

routePhases.description: Seoul → Sokcho via Yeongdong Expressway and Misiryeong Tunnel through the Taebaek mountains.
Free time inside Seoraksan National Park (Sogongwon entrance).
routePhases.summary: Seoraksan: Sinheungsa + Buda + cablecar a Gwongeumseong.

routePhases.description: Sokcho Fishery Market and Sokcho Beach, ≈15 minutes apart by road.
Coastal road then expressway back into Seoul.

routeShapeIntro.title: El recorrido del día
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: Salida muy temprana desde Seúl → 2,5 horas al Parque Nacional Seoraksan (cablecar + Sinheungsa) → Sokcho (marisco fresco + playa del Mar del Este) → 2,5 horas de regreso. Una jornada de 14-16 horas solo para los más activos.

bestFor: Amantes de la naturaleza que quieren el mejor parque nacional de Corea en un día
Fotógrafos (Seoraksan en follaje otoñal = la mejor foto de Corea)
Quienes quieren combinar montaña y playa en un mismo día
lessIdealFor: Quienes prefieren jornadas cortas (es un tour de 14-16 horas muy largo)
Familias con niños muy pequeños (cablecar + larga jornada)

practicalAccordionItems.title: What's included
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Round-trip coach transport from Seoul, English-speaking tour guide, Seoraksan National…
content: Round-trip coach transport from Seoul, English-speaking tour guide, Seoraksan National Park admission.
Not included: meals, drinks, optional Seoraksan cable car (~₩15,000), market food, tips.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Pickup & drop-off
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Hongik University Station (Exit 8) at ≈07:00 or Myeongdong Station (Exit 4) at ≈07:30.
content: Hongik University Station (Exit 8) at ≈07:00 or Myeongdong Station (Exit 4) at ≈07:30.
Arrive 10 minutes early.
There is no hotel pickup; meeting points only.
Latecomers cannot be accommodated.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Walking & terrain
content: Moderate.
Seoraksan's main path is paved and flat to the cable-car base; the Biseondae trail is a gentle 1.5 km each way.
The market is flat indoor walking.
Sokcho Beach is soft sand.
No serious hiking; bring comfortable closed-toe shoes.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What to wear & bring
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Comfortable walking shoes, layered clothing — Seoraksan is 5–10°C cooler than Seoul,…
content: Comfortable walking shoes, layered clothing — Seoraksan is 5–10°C cooler than Seoul, especially in autumn and winter.
Light jacket in autumn, warm coat in winter.
Camera, water, cash for cable car and market food (₩20,000–₩40,000 typical).
Card payment is widely accepted at the market.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Cancellation
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Free cancellation up to 24 hours before the tour starts.
content: Free cancellation up to 24 hours before the tour starts.
Same-day cancellations and no-shows are non-refundable.
If the operator cancels for weather (typhoon, heavy snow), you'll be offered a reschedule or refund.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Children & accessibility
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Children are welcome but the day is long (12 hours).
content: Children are welcome but the day is long (12 hours).
The market and beach are stroller-friendly; the Seoraksan paved entrance is too, but Biseondae trail is not.
Limited accessibility for wheelchair users on Seoraksan beyond the cable-car base.

practicalWeatherStatic._seasonalSummary: Sokcho and Seoraksan are noticeably cooler than Seoul, especially in autumn and winter. Coastal wind can make the beach feel colder than the temperature suggests.

_seasonalTemperatures.notes: Cherry blossoms around Yeongnang Lake in late April.
Stable hiking weather; beach is busy in July–August.
Peak foliage on Seoraksan ridges mid-Oct to early Nov.
Possible snow on the trails; cable car may close in high winds.

seasonalVariations.description: Seoraksan con flores de azalea y bosque verde. Sokcho con días templados.
Playa de Sokcho en temporada alta. Seoraksan fresco y verde.
**El mejor momento**: el follaje otoñal de Seoraksan es el más fotografiado de Corea (oct-nov).
Seoraksan nevado. El cablecar puede cerrarse con hielo. Jornada muy larga en frío.

bookingTrustItems.title: Grupo pequeño
bookingTrustItems.body: Minibús de grupo pequeño para mayor comodidad en la larga jornada.

bookingSupportSteps.title: Posventa
bookingSupportSteps.description: Post-tour feedback request; Gangwon Province travel questions answered.

staticQuestions.question: ¿El cablecar de Seoraksan está incluido?
staticQuestions.answer: La **entrada al parque ₩5.000** está incluida. El cablecar (~₩18.000-20.000) puede estar incluido o no según la reserva — confirme al reservar. En temporada alta (otoño) puede tener espera.

staticQuestions.question: ¿Cuánto senderismo hay?
staticQuestions.answer: El tour no incluye senderismo técnico. El cablecar lleva hasta los 800 m. Desde la estación superior, la Fortaleza Gwongeumseong es un paseo de 10 min. El Templo Sinheungsa es llano.

staticQuestions.question: ¿Qué hay que comer en el mercado de Sokcho?
staticQuestions.answer: Las especialidades son: Ojingeo-sun (calamar relleno de carne, ₩3.000-5.000), Dakgangjeong (pollo crujiente dulce-picante) y marisco fresco a la plancha (~₩20.000-40.000/persona en el piso superior).

staticQuestions.question: ¿Puedo bañarme en la Playa de Sokcho?
staticQuestions.answer: En verano (julio-agosto) el área de baño está habilitada. Fuera de temporada la playa es para pasear y fotografiar.

staticQuestions.question: (Consulte las preguntas frecuentes al reservar)
staticQuestions.answer: Para más información, contacte a AtoC Korea al reservar.

staticQuestions.question: ¿Qué pasa si llueve?
staticQuestions.answer: El tour sigue normalmente. El cablecar puede cerrar con viento fuerte — en ese caso, el tiempo extra se da en Sinheungsa y Sokcho. Con tifón: cancelación total con reembolso.

staticQuestions.question: (Consulte las preguntas frecuentes al reservar)
staticQuestions.answer: Para más información, contacte a AtoC Korea al reservar.

staticQuestions.question: ¿Difícil la caminata?
staticQuestions.answer: Ligero-moderado. Sin senderismo técnico. El recorrido dentro del parque (Sinheungsa + zona del cablecar) es plano o suave.

staticQuestions.question: ¿Adecuado para familias?
staticQuestions.answer: Sí para familias con niños de 8+ años cómodos con la larga jornada. El cablecar es especialmente popular con niños.

staticQuestions.question: ¿Personas mayores?
staticQuestions.answer: Para mayores activos sin problemas con las largas jornadas: sí. El cablecar evita la subida a pie.

staticQuestions.question: ¿Edad mínima?
staticQuestions.answer: A partir de 8 años para la jornada de 14-16 horas.

itineraryStops.name: Seoul departure
itineraryStops.description: Pickup from Hongik University Station (Exit 8) or Myeongdong Station (Exit 4). About 2.5 hours northeast by coach via the Yeongdong Expressway and Misiryeong Tunnel through the Taebaek mountains, into Gangwon Province and the East Sea coast.
highlights: Coach with rest stop en route
timeUsed: Meet driver-guide at Seoul pickup point
Confirm group, distribute water and snacks
Begin 2.5-hour drive to Seoraksan via Yangyang Expressway
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Hotel-area pickup keeps the day's logistics simple. Departing by 07:30 ensures you arrive at Seoraksan before the 10:30 cable car peak rush.

_poi_meta.note: Operational pickup stop — Seoul departure (variable hotel/station pickup); not a sightseeing POI

itineraryStops.name: Seoraksan National Park
itineraryStops.description: About three hours of free time at Korea's most-loved national park — its 5th national park (designated March 1970) and Korea's first UNESCO Biosphere Reserve (1982). From the Sogongwon main entrance you can walk the paved path past Sinheungsa Temple's Great Bronze Buddha to Biseondae Rock (1.5 km each way along a stream), or take the optional Gwongeumseong cable car (not included, ~₩15,000) for a high-ridge view.
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The 4-hour drive each way is justified only by the centerpiece: Korea's 5th national park (1970) and a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve since 1982. Picked over Naksansa-temple alternatives because Sogongwon entrance puts paved Sinheungsa + cable-car-ridge access + Biseondae stream trail within a single 3-hour window — no other Seoraksan stop combines all three with this short walking budget.

visitBasics.hours: Park always open; cable car typically 08:30–17:00 (weather-dependent)
visitBasics.closed: Open year-round; some trails close for restoration
visitBasics.admission: Park free; cable car: Adult 13,000 KRW / Child 9,000 KRW (round-trip)
visitBasics.walking: Varies — easy walks at base, moderate to challenging hikes (Heundeulbawi/Geumganggul require stairs and steep paths)

convenience.restroom: At main entrance area, cable car station, and along major trails

smartNotes.photo: Bronze Buddha at Sinheungsa; cable car platform with peak views; Heundeulbawi balanced rock
smartNotes.facilities: Visitor center, restaurants, gift shops, motels at park entrance
smartNotes.tip: October weekends are extremely crowded — book cable car in advance; Heundeulbawi hike is 1.5 hours each way; pair with Sokcho seafood market

_poi_meta.note: Operational details verified via established knowledge

itineraryStops.name: Sokcho Tourist Fishery Market
itineraryStops.description: A short drive to Sokcho's lively waterfront seafood market. The covered market is famous across Korea for sundae (blood sausage), live East Sea sashimi (hwe), king crab, and abai-style stuffed squid — a recipe brought south by Hamgyeong-province refugees who settled in nearby Abai Village after the Korean War. Free time to graze, sit down for a meal, or pick up market snacks.
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Anchors the day's food story: covered seafood market with East Sea sashimi, abai-style stuffed squid (a Korean War refugee dish from Hamgyeong-province settlers), and Sokcho dakgangjeong. Chosen over a generic restaurant lunch because it doubles as cultural context for Sokcho's port history and lets travelers graze rather than commit to one menu.

visitBasics.walking: Easy — covered market with concrete floor

smartNotes.photo: Manseok Dakgangjeong queue (often the longest line); colorful seafood displays
smartNotes.facilities: ATMs, multiple food stalls, sit-down restaurants on upper floors
smartNotes.tip: Manseok Dakgangjeong queue is famously long — go early or late; squid sundae is the local specialty

_poi_meta.note: Operational details based on aggregated visitor info

itineraryStops.name: Sokcho Beach
itineraryStops.description: A walk along Sokcho's main beach — a long stretch of golden sand looking out at the East Sea, with Seoraksan's silhouette visible behind the coastline. The Sokcho Eye ferris wheel sits at the south end. Optional time for the lighthouse observatory or Yeongnang Coastal Road if the group is moving fast.
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Caps the day with horizontal scale after Seoraksan's vertical: long flat sand, East Sea horizon, and Seoraksan's silhouette visible inland — the same coastline mountaineers see when descending. Kept short (60-90 min) because the 2.5-hour return drive sets a hard ceiling. Sokcho Eye ferris wheel and the lighthouse observatory are walkable optionals if the group moves fast.

visitBasics.hours: Open 24 hours; managed swim season July–August

smartNotes.photo: Sunrise over the East Sea; pine forest with beach in foreground
smartNotes.facilities: Café strip along the beach; convenience stores; adjacent fish market
smartNotes.tip: Combine with Sokcho Tourist Fishery Market for fresh seafood; Pokemon GO trainers historically congregated here

_poi_meta.note: Operational details based on aggregated visitor info

itineraryStops.name: Return to Seoul
itineraryStops.description: Coach back to Seoul, arriving in the early evening at the original meeting points. Drop-off at Hongik University Station and Myeongdong Station.
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Return logistics — drop-off chosen for guest convenience and timing certainty.

_poi_meta.note: Operational stop (return); identified by name pattern. visitBasics/convenience/smartNotes were not factsheet-derived.

routePhases.description: Seoul → Sokcho via Yeongdong Expressway and Misiryeong Tunnel through the Taebaek mountains.
Free time inside Seoraksan National Park (Sogongwon entrance).
Sokcho Fishery Market and Sokcho Beach, ≈15 minutes apart by road.
Coastal road then expressway back into Seoul.

routeShapeIntro.title: How this day flows
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: A mountain-then-sea linear loop east of Seoul. Three hours among Seoraksan's granite peaks in the morning, then a 30-minute drop down to the coast for a market lunch and a walk on Sokcho Beach. Two of Korea's most distinctive landscapes — the country's iconic national park and its friendliest east-coast port — without overnight planning.

bestFor: Active travelers comfortable with full days
lessIdealFor: Travelers needing a tight same-day flight buffer

routeLogicSections.title: Why this route is built this way

items.label: More mountain time than the standard tour
items.detail: Three hours at Seoraksan is enough to walk to Biseondae and ride the cable car, not just glance at the entrance. Many east-coast tours pair Seoraksan with three other stops and leave only 90 minutes for the park itself.

items.label: Real Sokcho food, not a tourist meal
items.detail: The Tourist Fishery Market is where Sokcho actually eats — Hamgyeong-style refugee dishes, East Sea sashimi, dakgangjeong. Free time means you choose your own bowl rather than getting a fixed bus-tour lunch.

items.label: Cleaner pairing than mountain-plus-Nami-Island
items.detail: Other east-bound day tours combine Seoraksan with Nami Island or the Garden of Morning Calm for a 13–14 hour day. This itinerary stays on the Sokcho axis — mountain, market, beach — for a tighter, less-rushed mood.

items.label: Year-round value
items.detail: Autumn foliage is the headline season, but the same route works for summer beach days, winter snow ridges, and spring greens. The market is busy in any weather, and the coach drive cuts out the train-and-bus transfer that DIY visitors spend half their day on.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What's included
practicalAccordionItems.content: Round-trip coach transport from Seoul, English-speaking tour guide, Seoraksan National Park admission. Not included: meals, drinks, optional Seoraksan cable car (~₩15,000), market food, tips.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Pickup & drop-off
practicalAccordionItems.content: Hongik University Station (Exit 8) at ≈07:00 or Myeongdong Station (Exit 4) at ≈07:30. Arrive 10 minutes early. There is no hotel pickup; meeting points only. Latecomers cannot be accommodated.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Walking & terrain
practicalAccordionItems.content: Moderate. Seoraksan's main path is paved and flat to the cable-car base; the Biseondae trail is a gentle 1.5 km each way. The market is flat indoor walking. Sokcho Beach is soft sand. No serious hiking; bring comfortable closed-toe shoes.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What to wear & bring
practicalAccordionItems.content: Comfortable walking shoes, layered clothing — Seoraksan is 5–10°C cooler than Seoul, especially in autumn and winter. Light jacket in autumn, warm coat in winter. Camera, water, cash for cable car and market food (₩20,000–₩40,000 typical). Card payment is widely accepted at the market.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Cancellation
practicalAccordionItems.content: Free cancellation up to 24 hours before the tour starts. Same-day cancellations and no-shows are non-refundable. If the operator cancels for weather (typhoon, heavy snow), you'll be offered a reschedule or refund.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Children & accessibility
practicalAccordionItems.content: Children are welcome but the day is long (12 hours). The market and beach are stroller-friendly; the Seoraksan paved entrance is too, but Biseondae trail is not. Limited accessibility for wheelchair users on Seoraksan beyond the cable-car base.

practicalWeatherStatic._seasonalSummary: Sokcho and Seoraksan are noticeably cooler than Seoul, especially in autumn and winter. Coastal wind can make the beach feel colder than the temperature suggests.

_seasonalTemperatures.notes: Cherry blossoms around Yeongnang Lake in late April.
Stable hiking weather; beach is busy in July–August.
Peak foliage on Seoraksan ridges mid-Oct to early Nov.
Possible snow on the trails; cable car may close in high winds.

seasonalVariations.highlight: Fresh greens on Seoraksan, cherry blossoms around Yeongnang Lake in Sokcho. Quietest of the four seasons.
Sokcho Beach is open for swimming (typically July–August); Seoraksan trails are forested and shaded.
Korea's most photographed foliage on the Seoraksan ridges, mid-Oct to early Nov. Tour books out fast in this window.
Snow-dusted granite peaks, fewer crowds, hot abai-soondae at the market. Cable car may close on high-wind days.

bookingTrustItems.title: Licensed local operator
bookingTrustItems.description: Authorized Korean tour operator with full insurance

bookingTrustItems.description: Hand-tuned itinerary, not a generic loop

staticQuestions.question: Is the Seoraksan cable car included?
staticQuestions.answer: No. The cable car to Gwongeumseong Fortress is optional and costs around ₩15,000 round-trip on the day. You can buy tickets at the base station during free time. The cable car may close in high winds or maintenance days.

staticQuestions.question: How much hiking is involved?
staticQuestions.answer: None is required. The standard route is paved walking from the park entrance to Sinheungsa Temple and the cable-car base (≈15 minutes). The optional Biseondae trail is a flat 1.5 km each way. Heavy hiking (Ulsanbawi, Daecheongbong) needs an overnight in Sokcho, not a day tour.

staticQuestions.question: What's good to eat at the market?
staticQuestions.answer: East Sea sashimi (hwe) at the market's seafood floors; abai-style stuffed squid (ojingeo-sundae); Sokcho dakgangjeong (sweet-spicy fried chicken); fresh king crab in season. Budget ₩20,000–₩40,000 per person.

staticQuestions.question: Can I swim at Sokcho Beach?
staticQuestions.answer: The beach swimming season is typically July to August. Outside this window, the beach is for walking and photos only. The water is cold even in early summer.

staticQuestions.question: How is this different from the Mt Seorak + Naksan Temple tour?
staticQuestions.answer: This tour stays in Sokcho city — mountain + market + beach. The Naksan Temple version drops the market and trades the beach for Naksansa, a 1,300-year-old clifftop Buddhist temple 25 minutes south in Yangyang. Choose this one for the Sokcho food/coast experience; the Naksansa version for Buddhist heritage.

staticQuestions.question: What if it rains?
staticQuestions.answer: The tour runs in light rain. The market is fully covered and the Seoraksan path is mostly tree-shaded. In a typhoon or heavy snow, the operator may cancel and offer a reschedule or refund.

staticQuestions.question: Is this a good choice for first-time visitors to Seoraksan & Sokcho?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — this is the most efficient way to combine Seoraksan and the East Sea coast in a single guided day. The 12-hour day with 2.5 hours each way means a long drive, but the trade-off is hitting Korea's most-loved national park, Sokcho's seafood market, and the Sokcho beach in one trip — visits that otherwise take 2-3 days from Seoul.

staticQuestions.question: Is the walking difficult?
staticQuestions.answer: Moderate. Seoraksan's paved core (Sogongwon entrance to Sinheungsa Temple and the cable-car base) is flat for ≈600 m round trip. Optional Biseondae trail adds 1.5 km each way along a stream (flat, well-marked). The market is covered with concrete floors. Sokcho Beach is loose sand for ≈1 km. The cable car deck has stairs but is short. Total walking ≈4-5 km if you do everything; ≈2 km if you skip Biseondae.

staticQuestions.question: Is this tour suitable for families?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes for families whose kids can handle a 12-hour day with 5 hours of driving. The Seoraksan paved path is stroller-tolerant and the market is fully covered (good wet-weather backup). The seafood is an experience for older kids and teens; younger kids may prefer the dakgangjeong (sweet-spicy fried chicken). The beach is flat sand for play. Bring snacks for the long drive each way.

staticQuestions.question: Can older adults join comfortably?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes for active seniors comfortable with the long drive. The Seoraksan paved core is flat and stroller-tolerant; the cable car (optional, ₩15,000) skips the climb to the ridge. The covered market has chairs at the seafood floors. Beach walking is flat but loose sand can tire mobility-limited visitors — the Yeongnang Coastal Road is a paved alternative. Less ideal if a 5-hour total drive is uncomfortable.

staticQuestions.question: What's the recommended minimum age?
staticQuestions.answer: Best fit is around age 6 and up — the day involves a 2.5-hour coach drive each way, ≈3 hours of free time at Seoraksan, and managing food choices at a busy seafood market. Younger kids manage with snacks and rest breaks. The cable car can be tense for kids with motion sensitivity. The beach is the easy win — bring sand toys in summer.

v17_schema_unification.description: Phase 1: Converted matching_profile scores from 1-5 int scale to 0-1 float scale to match the 3 Step 3 new tours. Mapping 1→0.20, 2→0.40, 3→0.60, 4→0.80, 5→1.00. Mechanical conversion preserves ordinal information; verified-fact recalibration happens in subsequent enhancement batches.

v17_batch9_enhancement.description: Batch 9 deep enhancement — Seoul-Seoraksan-Sokcho + Pocheon Sanjeong/Herb/Art Valley pair. Single-POI fixed-itinerary methodology (batches 1-5, 8 style). Both tours are SMALL_GROUP — formula different from batch 7/8 private tours. All substantive stops enhanced with 1,000+ char descriptions weaving verified facts. matching_profile recalibrated to verified-fact 0-1 float scores. Charter-format dimensions previously null: private_tour_fit set to 0.05 (definitionally not private); customizable_route_fit set to 0.1 (small-group fixed itinerary); flexibility_fit set to 0.3 (small-group bus, fixed pickup/drop). Tour-specific: history_culture_fit Seoraksan 0.5 (Sinheungsa 652 + Gwongeumseong 1253 + Tongildaebul 1997) / Pocheon 0.4 (1925 reservoir + quarry restoration). Seoraksan tour: unesco_fit 0.2 → 0.4 (UNESCO Biosphere Reserve since 1982, Korea's only one); autumn_foliage_fit set to 1.0 (catalog-leading); mountain_fit set to 1.0 (catalog-leading); seasonal_fit 0.95. Pocheon tour: romantic_fit set to 0.85 (catalog-second-strongest after Nami tour 0.95); seasonal_fit 0.85; family_fit 0.8 → 0.85. 6 new POIs added to KB (v1.11 → v1.12): seoraksan_national_park, sokcho_tourist_fishery_market, sokcho_beach, sanjeong_lake, herb_island, pocheon_art_valley. KB POI count: 62 → 68.

_score_audit._methodology: 0-1 float scale per Phase 1 standard. Both tours are SMALL_GROUP (not private) — formula different from Batch 7/8 private tours. Charter-format dimensions previously null on these tours: private_tour_fit set to 0.05 (definitionally not private); customizable_route_fit 0.1 (small-group fixed itinerary - no menu of routes); flexibility_fit 0.3 (small-group bus, fixed pickup/drop, fixed stops). history_culture_fit set per content: Seoraksan tour 0.5 (Sinheungsa Silla 652 + Gwongeumseong 1253 legend); Pocheon tour 0.4 (Sanjeong 1925 reservoir + Art Valley quarry-history). Time-weighted indoor_ratio re-computed per Fix 1 methodology using verified per-POI indoor_ratio_at_stop values.

private_tour_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED — both tours are small-group bus/coach tours, definitionally not private. Was unset (None).

customizable_route_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED — small-group fixed itinerary; no route options. Was unset.

flexibility_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED — small-group format; fixed pickup/drop times, fixed stops, fixed pacing. Was unset. Lower than batch 7/8 private (0.6-0.8).

history_culture_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED — Sinheungsa Temple founded 652 (Silla) + Gwongeumseong Fortress 1253 Mongolian-invasion legend + Tongildaebul 1997 reunification monument. Substantial culture, but mountain nature dominates. 0.5 calibrated.

unesco_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED higher — Seoraksan IS a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve since August 1982 (Korea's only one). NOT a World Heritage Site (only on tentative list). Calibrated up to 0.4 — Biosphere Reserve carries weight but isn't the World Heritage gold standard.

iconic_landmark_fit.reasoning: Verified maintained — Korea's #1 most-loved national park, world's largest seated bronze Buddha. Maintained at 1.0.

first_time_fit.reasoning: Verified — strongest first-time mountain + East Sea single-day option. Slight upward to 0.85.

walking_level.reasoning: VERIFIED — Seoraksan paths are paved at base (Sinheungsa, cable-car base) but Biseondae is 3 km walking; cable car eliminates the steep hike; Sokcho stops are flat. Time-weighted ~0.65.

indoor_ratio.reasoning: VERIFIED — Fix 1 time-weighted: Seoul departure 15 × 1.0 (transit) = 15, Seoraksan 180 × 0.05 = 9, Sokcho Market 75 × 0.5 = 37.5, Sokcho Beach 75 × 0.0 = 0, Lunch 30 × 0.9 = 27, Return 150 × 1.0 = 150, transit-other 195 × 1.0 = 195. Total indoor 433.5 / 720 min = 0.60 with full transit; substantive only 73.5 / 360 = 0.20. Catalog value 0.15 (slight upward from prior 0.10 — better reflects market interior weight).

weather_sensitivity.reasoning: Verified — Seoraksan cable car closes in high wind / heavy rain / fog (no view); Sokcho Beach is fully exposed; Sokcho Market is the only weather-protected fallback. Heavy-outdoor day. Slight markdown to 0.9 (very high but Sokcho Market provides partial fallback).

scenic_level.reasoning: Verified maintained — Korea's most iconic mountain + East Sea coast. Maintained at 1.0.

photo_level.reasoning: Verified maintained — Tongildaebul Buddha + cable-car panorama + East Sea + Sokcho Eye Ferris Wheel. Maintained at 1.0.

nature_fit.reasoning: Verified maintained — Korea's first UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. Maintained at 1.0.

autumn_foliage_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED — Seoraksan autumn foliage (October-early November) is Korea's most famous foliage destination, with cable-car-framed granite peaks. Was unset. Maximum 1.0.

mountain_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED — Daecheongbong 1,708m (Korea's 3rd highest), 30 peaks, Ulsanbawi granite formation. Catalog-leading mountain content. Was unset. Maximum 1.0.

seasonal_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED — autumn foliage peak Oct-Nov is the iconic season; spring azaleas + summer green + winter snow each have distinct appeals. Was unset. 0.95 calibrated.

beach_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED — Sokcho Beach is one of Korea's representative East Sea beaches (75-min stop). Slight upward from 0.8 to 0.85 — beach is included but not the day's centerpiece (Seoraksan is).

food_fit.reasoning: Verified maintained — Sokcho Tourist Fishery Market is Gangwon's signature food market (dakgangjeong, squid sundae, abai sundae). Maintained at 1.0.

small_group_fit.reasoning: Verified maintained — small-group coach format with driver-guide. Maintained at 1.0.

bus_fit.reasoning: Verified maintained — coach vehicle. Maintained at 1.0.

premium_fit.reasoning: Verified maintained — small-group coach with shared pickup is mid-tier value proposition. Maintained at 0.4.

value_for_money_fit.reasoning: Verified — 12-hour day covering UNESCO Biosphere mountain + signature market + East Sea beach at small-group pricing is strong value. Slight upward from 0.8 to 0.85.

storytelling_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED higher — Sinheungsa Silla founding (652 CE), Gwongeumseong 1253 Mongolian-invasion legend, Tongildaebul 1997 reunification monument, Sokcho Abai Village Korean War refugee heritage. Driver-guide narrative is rich. Calibrated up to 0.75.

family_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED — 12-hour day with optional steep hikes is moderate for families. Cable car + market + beach all family-friendly. Slight upward to 0.65.
